# split_h_2p_demo SPRITE STRESS RAIL — Audit Protocol audit-1

**Branch/commits audited:** `task2-rail`, 6 commits on top of `claude/split-screen-rotation-smoothness-vu13t7` @ `11791aa`
(`408a1a4` inc-0 core · `9b0a6f6` inc-1 input · `c3dfba5` inc-2 AI · `dfb6e5c` inc-3 tiers · `78c9349` inc-4 sweep · `46cdd84` inc-5 docs; 22 files, +3,507/−1).
**Role:** independent AUDIT-1 — no part in the implementation or the trial. All evidence below regenerated by me from a fresh materialization (`dryrun_split.sh /tmp/kit_a1t2`), all measurements on the cycle-accurate emulator via MesenRunner; framebuffer-first (screenshot y = PPU + 7 modeled), WRAM only for the sanctioned motion/cadence class.
**Method:** fresh materialize → build default + all 25 variants → run both suites → full-diff read of `sprites_2p.inc` (1,902 lines) / `main.asm` delta / tests / generator / build script / docs → adversarial re-measurement of every headline number (cadence grid, instrument slopes, glue proof at MY OWN pin pair with freshly built ROMs, overflow forensics, seam guard, input determinism with MY OWN script) → 5 non-vacuity controls run by hand → asset regeneration byte-compare → gates ×4 with this report present in the tree.

## VERDICT: FINDINGS — remediate (one MEDIUM, test-hygiene only)

Every measured claim in the increment reproduced from scratch on the emulator — the
per-sprite costs within 1%, the cadence curve at 6 grid points including both break
points, the glue proof at a heading pair the implementation never saw, the 8-per-row
sliver ceiling exactly, the seam guard band white-free, and byte-identical two-port
input trajectories against the integer model. The rail's mechanism is sound and the
test surface is honest. The one remediation item is a latent flake **in the shipped
glue test itself**: it reads the `$7E:E400` debug mirrors while the game loop
free-runs, and I caught the `$7FFF` culled-sentinel mid-rewrite twice during this
audit (parked re-read confirmed the ASM value is exact — the math is right, the
sampling is racy). It is the implementer's own "free-running WRAM sampling races"
paper cut, applied to their own test. Two-line fix; everything else is accept/info.

## 1. Suite runs (verbatim, from /tmp/kit_a1t2)

```
$ python -m pytest tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py tests/test_gen_pose_tables.py -q
......................................                                   [100%]
38 passed in 89.63s (0:01:29)

$ python -m pytest tests/test_split_h_demo.py tests/test_split_h_matrix_demo.py \
    tests/test_split_h_persp_demo.py tests/test_split_h_persp3_demo.py \
    tests/test_split_v_demo.py tests/test_split_v_fight.py -q
..................x...................................                   [100%]
53 passed, 1 xfailed in 75.64s (0:01:15)
```

Both match the claims exactly (38 = 27 rail + 11 tooling; 53+1 xfail split family).

**Documented flake** (`test_rot256_bank_delivery_render_period`, 0/1-frame
capture-lag class): did **not** reproduce — passed in the family run and 3×
in isolation (`1 passed in 5.19s / 5.22s / 5.08s`). 0/4 this session →
**stays documented, no fix needed.**

## 3. Adversarial re-measurements (theirs vs mine — all mine from fresh boots this session)

| Measurement | Their claim | My re-measurement | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| Shipped default N=24 cadence | lockstep | **lockstep ×40 stepped frames** (loop+40/NMI+40), both players turning+driving | ✓ (stronger window than the suite's 24) |
| All-visible ceiling (spr_pinvis) | lockstep 24, breaks 32 | N=24 lockstep ×24; N=32 **breaks at step 2** (loop+1/NMI+2) | ✓ |
| Integrated curve (spr_sweep) | 32 lockstep, 48+ break | N=32 lockstep ×24; N=48 **breaks at step 2** | ✓ |
| Alt-frame probe | doubles ceiling 32→64 | N=64 lockstep ×24 on `spr_alt`; suite curve JSON: full breaks at 48, alt locks through 64, breaks 96 | ✓ |
| Visible cost | 5,595 mc/sprite/band | **5,653** (slope N=8→64 ÷ 2) | ✓ +1.0% |
| v-culled cost | 2,003 | **1,986** | ✓ −0.8% |
| Chebyshev-culled cost | 606 | **603** | ✓ −0.5% |
| AI cost | 6,074 mc/follower/frame | **6,019** (slope over 56 followers) | ✓ −0.9% |
| Integrated tick % frame | 24→58%, 32→75% | 24→**56.2%**, 32→**76.1%**; headroom model **33%/10%** vs claimed 31%/10% | ✓ |
| Glue proof | 3 pins + control at pins searched by the generator | **at MY pin pair (h1=128, h2=21)** — chosen by my own scan, never seen by the implementation; freshly built `a1_pin.sfc`/`a1_pinfwd.sfc`: **15/15** interior mirror-predicted spots show white on the render; SP_FORWARD control at the same pins: **7/7 discriminating spots MISS** | ✓ non-vacuous at unseen headings |
| ASM-vs-Python mirror drift | ≤1 px | parked read: **0 px** at every checked spot (incl. the one that read `$7FFF` free-running — see Finding 1) | ✓ (with the race caveat) |
| Overflow forensics | exactly 8 of ~30 drawn on the k=78 row | **8 of 30** fully drawn | ✓ exact |
| ±16-row neighbour damage | overloaded row eats box-span neighbours | in-span ladder discs (box rows 62-93): rows 64/75/87 **eaten**, 69/81 drawn; **zero** missing discs outside the span (row 64 = 14 rows above the loaded row — the transparent-box sliver drain confirmed) | ✓ (damage is positional within the span, not total — consistent with per-line sliver order) |
| Seam guard rows 111-112 | white-free on shipped default | **0 / 0 / 0** white px across 3 stepped captures at 14-frame hops with both players driving | ✓ |
| Input determinism | 2 runs byte-identical, == model | MY 25-frame script: run1 == run2 **byte-identical**, == integer model exactly (first-divergence check: none); final state h1=249 h2=126 | ✓ |

## 4. Test-surface honesty (10 new tests)

| Test | Surface | Name vs assertions | Control (same metric?) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `test_sp_input_determinism_two_ports` | WRAM camera state (sanctioned motion class, labeled) | honest | input-scramble flips the trajectory — **run by me: diverges ✓** |
| `test_sp_player_marker_renders_in_other_band` | framebuffer (magenta patch at mirror-predicted spot) | honest | own-band zero-magenta is the built-in flip of the same count |
| `test_sp_ai_followers_exact_model_and_ncap` | WRAM entity block (motion class, labeled) | honest — asserts the full 6-field state == model at exactly F ticks, 2 boots; N-cap freeze | divergence-by-construction (any steering/frac error) |
| `test_sp_ai_reaches_waypoints_bounded_and_renders` | framebuffer + WRAM wp indices | honest | 3 far-spot zero-white checks, same classifier |
| `test_sp_tier_ladder_extents_and_boundaries` | framebuffer through-centre extents | honest — all 5 tiers, ≤1-step transitions | SP_TIEROFF — **run by me: width spread 12 → 0 ✓** |
| `test_sp_seam_margin_cull_and_culloff_control` | framebuffer exact rows 111-112 (+7 modeled) | honest | SP_CULLOFF — **run by me: 0 → 74 white px ✓** |
| `test_sp_cadence_sweep_curve` | WRAM counter pair (THE sanctioned cadence oracle) | honest — durable asserts 24/32 lock, 128 break (gate non-vacuity), alt-64 lock; border points recorded | 128-break IS the metric's own non-vacuity — **break reads reproduced by me at pinvis-32 and sweep-48 ✓** |
| `test_sp_overflow_row_forensics` | framebuffer centre-presence vs mirror intent | honest (5..11 tolerance band around the 8 model; I measured exactly 8) | sparse-row zero-drop, same metric, box-span-aware window |
| `test_sp_shipped_default_gate` | framebuffer white presence + masks + SP_OVER + cadence | honest | composite of already-controlled metrics |
| `test_sp_glue_proof_pinned_and_forward_control` | framebuffer patches at mirror-predicted spots | honest (mirror drift explicitly labeled diagnostic) | SP_FORWARD wrong-matrix — **run by me at MY OWN pins: 7/7 miss ✓** |

**AI exact-model oracle-gap assessment (asked for explicitly):** `_sp_ai_model`
re-implements the steering **logic** in Python but reads the **same committed
data tables** (`sp_way.bin`, `sp_world_main.bin`, `move256.bin`) the ROM
incbins — so a shared misconception in the steering convention could satisfy
the model test on both sides. The gap is closed behaviorally by
`test_sp_ai_reaches_waypoints_bounded_and_renders`: waypoint **arrival**
measured live on the emulator (wp indices advance within 1000 real frames —
arrival is convention-independent; a wrong steering sense orbits away) plus
white discs on the render at mirror-predicted spots. Adequate; no proxy-only
assertion exists. (Note the data tables themselves are seeded content —
"wrong data" is not a meaningful failure mode for them, and the generator +
provenance gate pin them byte-exactly.)

Controls run by me this session: **5** (SP_FORWARD at unseen pins, SP_CULLOFF,
SP_TIEROFF, input-scramble, cadence-gate break reads) — every one flips the
exact metric its paired assertion reads.

## 5. Deviations 1-8 (ledger lives in the session report, not the tree — mapping below reconstructed from the commit trail; see Finding 4)

| # | Deviation | Evaluation |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 8-bit multiply core delivered 1.52× (not the estimated ~3×); honest number kept, FastROM named as the untouched lever | **Accept.** Re-measured (5,653 vs trial 8,522 = 1.51×). The cause analysis (slow-ROM fetch tail dominates) is sound and is now a paper cut — the right artifact |
| 2 | All-visible ceiling 24 (breaks 32), not the trial's 16 — ship default derived by the lockstep+headroom rule | **Accept.** Both points re-measured; instrument prediction (25.8) consistent |
| 3 | Pin/glue builds run SPRITES=128 (not 32) — band-2 coverage starved at 32 | **Accept.** At my own pins band-2 had 7 interior spots at N=128; compaction makes the cost identical (16 visible ⇒ 16 slots) |
| 4 | SP_CYCINT tick excludes pads/stamps (~1k mc) | **Accept.** Disclosed in-source; the headroom model adds 7k mc for VBlank commits on top of tick/0.86 — my re-computed headroom matches the shipped numbers |
| 5 | Raw cross-boot equal-tick assert REPLACED by per-boot exact-model match (×2 boots, 60+ ticks) | **Accept — strictly stronger.** Follower state is a pure function of tick count; matching the model at each boot's own tick count proves reproducibility without the wall-clock coin flip (`load_rom(run_seconds=…)` parks at 67 vs 68 frames across boots — the paper cut documents it). An equal-tick raw compare would re-introduce flake for zero added power |
| 6 | Makefile `LINT_ASM` gains `templates/*/*.inc` | **Accept — collision surface verified nil.** The glob matches exactly ONE file today (`sprites_2p.inc`) — checked across `task0-audit-1`, `task1-audit-1/2`, `task1-perband-256`, `task0-pr223-docs-honest`, `origin/claude/persp-c-horiz-review-ntqlca`, `origin/main`: **no other branch carries a non-assets `templates/*/*.inc`**. If a parallel branch later adds one, the effect is fail-loud at merge (new lint findings vs baseline), not silent — a gate-coverage improvement, not a hazard. width-check clean at 190 files |
| 7 | `test_rot256_bank_delivery_render_period` documented as a 0/1-frame capture-lag flake | **Accept.** 0/4 reproductions this session (§1); the pigeonhole design (min over 3 wrap-aligned captures) is the right shape; keep documented |
| 8 | Sweep border points (48/64/96) recorded to JSON, not asserted | **Accept.** The durable asserts pin everything ship-relevant (24, 32, 128-break, alt-64); I independently asserted the 48-break myself and it holds. Recording the full grid keeps the curve reproducible without making the suite hostage to border-point drift |

$ bash tools/cleanroom_check.sh

## 7. Findings

1. **[MEDIUM — fix] The glue test reads the `$7E:E400` mirrors while the game
   loop free-runs — a latent flake caught live twice in this audit.**
   `tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py:1440-1445` (`test_sp_glue_proof_pinned_and_forward_control`)
   reads the per-frame-rewritten mirror block with no `frame_stepping()`. The
   projection loop writes a `$7FFF` sentinel at the top of every entity
   iteration (`sprites_2p.inc:483-493`) and the real value ~5.6k mc later; a
   read landing in that window trips the `abs(msx − sx) <= 1` assert with a
   32,641-px "drift". I hit the sentinel twice in ~60 free-running mirror
   reads (band-1 entity 127 and a band-2 entity), and the parked re-read of
   the same address returned the exact predicted value (94,57) — **the ASM is
   correct; the sampling is racy.** This is the implementer's own
   "free-running WRAM sampling races" paper cut biting their own shipping
   test. **Fix (two lines):** wrap the mirror-read loop in
   `runner.frame_stepping()` (or read twice and keep the non-sentinel value).
   The render oracle — the part that decides pass/fail on geometry — is
   unaffected.
2. **[LOW — accept, note] SP_ALTFRAME's even-N contract is comment-only.**
   `sprites_2p.inc:866` computes `HCNT = SP_N >> 1`; an odd poked N leaves the
   last entity never projected and never AI-ticked, silently. All shipped
   pokes are even and the build is an owner-feel-test probe, not the default.
   If the probe is promoted, add a guard or round-up.
3. **[INFO] Duplicated routine banner.** `sprites_2p.inc:443-460` carries two
   consecutive header comment blocks for `sp_project_band` (an editing
   leftover; the second is the accurate one). Cosmetic.
4. **[INFO] The deviations ledger (1-8) is not in the tree** — it lives in the
   session report; §5's numbering is my reconstruction from the commit trail
   (items 5/6/7 confirmed by the audit brief's own references). Consider
   pasting the ledger into the audit-2 doc or the roadmap row next time so
   audits don't have to reconstruct it.
5. **[INFO] ±255 clamp quantified (adversarial probe, closes the question):**
   at cardinal headings the clamp shifts the projected row for 14 of 176
   distances, max 1 far-field row, ≤1 px in x; the lone visible↔cull flip
   (d=169) lands in rows the tier margins cull anyway. No render-observable
   defect; the docs' "quantization invisible at these sizes" claim stands.
6. **[INFO] Overflow "±16-row damage" is positional, not total** — within the
   loaded row's 32-px box span some discs survive (my probe: rows 64/75/87
   eaten, 69/81 drawn, nothing outside the span missing). The guide's wording
   ("damages ±16 neighbouring rows") is directionally right and the test's
   box-span-aware control window is correctly conservative; no change needed.

## 8. Verdict detail

- **Mechanism:** CLEAN — every core claim independently reproduced, including
  at heading pairs and input scripts the implementation never saw.
- **Numbers:** CLEAN — all within 1% of claims; curve verdicts identical at
  every re-measured grid point.
- **Test surface:** CLEAN except Finding 1 (a race in a diagnostic assert
  inside an otherwise framebuffer-first test).
- **Gates:** ×4 clean with this report present.

**Remediation scope for audit-2:** Finding 1 (park the mirror reads). Findings
2-6 are accept/info.

`a1_pinfwd.png`, `a1_overflow.png`, `a1_seam_0..2.png`, `a1_tier.png`,
`a1_tieroff.png`, `a1_culloff.png`; audit pin ROMs built as
`build/a1_pin.sfc` / `build/a1_pinfwd.sfc` (SP_H1=128 SP_H2=21).*
